meta.title: The Clever Monkey — Vocabulary Set 1
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: Vocabulary quiz for 'The Clever Monkey' — all 16 words from Weeks 1–4 (jungle, tricky, piece, cheese, mistake, fortune, divide, equal, fairly, swung, thanked, believe, afraid, exactly, carefully, sadly).

questions.question: What does 'jungle' mean?
options: A thick tropical forest full of animals
questions.explanation: A 'jungle' is a thick tropical forest. The Clever Monkey story takes place in a jungle in West Africa.

questions.question: What does 'fortune' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Fortune' means very good luck. The cats 'could not believe their good fortune' — finding the cheese was like a lucky surprise.

questions.question: 'Tricky' means something that is hard to do or sneaky.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Tricky' means difficult or sneaky. The monkey was very tricky — he used a clever plan to eat the cats' cheese.

questions.question: The monkey offered to _______ the cheese into two equal parts.
questions.hint: To cut or split into parts
questions.explanation: 'Divide' means to cut or split into parts. The monkey said he would divide the cheese — but he used it as a trick to eat it!

questions.question: What does 'mistake' mean?
options: An error — something that went wrong
questions.explanation: A 'mistake' is an error — something that went wrong. The monkey said 'I have made a mistake' each time he pretended the pieces were not equal.

questions.question: We have an _______ number of cookies. We each get the same amount.
questions.explanation: 'Equal' means the same amount or size. If everyone gets the same number of cookies, the amount is equal.

questions.question: The monkey 'swung' down from the tree. 'Swung' is the past tense of _______.
questions.hint: The monkey does this on tree branches
questions.explanation: 'Swung' is the past tense of 'swing.' The monkey swung — moved back and forth holding a branch — to come down from the tree.

questions.question: When you 'believe' something, you think it is true.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Believe' means to think something is true. The cats 'could not believe their good fortune' — they were so surprised that it was hard to think it was really true.

questions.question: I walk _______ down the icy stairs so I don't fall.
questions.explanation: 'Carefully' means doing something with great attention and care. Walking carefully on ice means paying close attention to each step.

questions.question: The cats looked _______ at their tiny pieces of cheese.
questions.explanation: 'Sadly' means in an unhappy way. The cats were sad because the monkey had eaten almost all their cheese.

questions.question: The monkey checked the cheese 'exactly.' Which sentence uses 'exactly' correctly?
options: The two pieces are exactly the same size — no difference at all.
questions.explanation: 'Exactly' means perfectly, with no difference at all. 'Exactly the same size' means both pieces are perfectly equal.

questions.question: Sort the vocabulary words by their part of speech.
questions.explanation: 'Jungle,' 'mistake,' and 'piece' are things (nouns). 'Divide' is an action (verb). 'Fairly' and 'sadly' describe how something is done (adverbs).

questions.question: I am _______ of the dark, so I always sleep with a small light on.
questions.explanation: 'Afraid' means feeling scared. The monkey used 'I am afraid' as a trick — he pretended to be sorry for eating too much cheese.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The cats thanked the monkey.' 'Thanked' is the past tense of 'thank' — the cats said thank you to the monkey each time he divided the cheese.

questions.question: 'Fairly,' 'carefully,' 'exactly,' and 'sadly' are all adverbs because they describe HOW something is done.
questions.explanation: Yes! All four words are adverbs. 'Fairly' (in a fair way), 'carefully' (with great care), 'exactly' (in a perfect way), and 'sadly' (in an unhappy way) — each describes how an action is done.
